source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A \( 20.0 \, \Omega \) resistor is embedded in a large block of ice at \( 0.00^\circ \text{C} \), and the battery has negligible internal resistance. At what rate (in g/s) is this circuit melting the ice? (The latent heat of fusion for ice is 3.34 \times 10^5 \, \text{J/kg}.)

asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Solving likely requires extracting circuit information from the image, then computing electrical power, then converting power to melt rate using latent heat of fusion
The task combines physics relations across domains: circuit power and phase-change energy
Answer is a numeric rate with units, suggesting a calculation pipeline rather than direct lookup
verifiability: Multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
The expected output is a specific numeric value with units (g/s), which is easy to validate once the circuit parameters are identified
registry_passed=true, so required assets appear available for downstream verification
